domain: Sumo basho (Japanese sumo grand tournament schedule): 6 tournaments per year in odd-numbered months across 4 host cities — Tokyo (Jan/May/Sep, 3x), Osaka (Mar), Nagoya (Jul), Fukuoka (Nov). 15-day tournaments. Weighted rotation with Tokyo central + 3 peripheral cities.
specific_mechanism: Fixed pre-scheduled weighted rotation through K=4 host venues: central venue gets 3/6 rounds, 3 peripheral venues get 1/6 each. Deterministic by calendar month.
llm_application: BASHO-ROTATION weighted fixed-schedule coordinator rotation: in an N-agent LLM system, designate K=4 candidate-coordinator agents. Per round r, select coordinator c = schedule[r mod 6] where schedule = [c_0, c_1, c_0, c_2, c_0, c_3] (Tokyo-Osaka-Tokyo-Nagoya-Tokyo-Fukuoka pattern); thus c_0 is selected on 50% of rounds, c_1/c_2/c_3 each on 1/6. All other N-K agents communicate only via the current coordinator c. Differs from (a) RECONCILE round-table (no central, every agent votes), (b) MAD debate (no fixed coordinator schedule), (c) round-robin uniform leader (no central weighting), (d) random leader-election (probabilistic not pre-scheduled), (e) A2A protocol (peer-to-peer no fixed coordinator). Recombines (i) FIXED PRE-SCHEDULED rotation + (ii) WEIGHTED CENTRAL leader + (iii) K=4 SMALL candidate pool.
novelty_claim: RECONCILE no central. MAD no fixed schedule. Round-robin uniform. Random leader-election probabilistic. BASHO-ROTATION recombines (a) FIXED pre-scheduled rotation + (b) WEIGHTED CENTRAL leader + (c) K=4 candidate pool. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
